name: Bob Brookmeyer
description: American jazz valve trombonist, pianist, arranger, and composer (1929–2011)
abstract: Robert Edward "Bob" Brookmeyer (December 19, 1929 – December 15, 2011) was an American jazz valve trombonist, pianist, arranger, and composer. Born in Kansas City, Missouri, Brookmeyer first gained widespread public attention as a member of Gerry Mulligan's quartet from 1954 to 1957.

concerts.label: Midnight Performance: New Jazz at Carnegie
Ninth Annual WLIB Festival of Negro Music and Drama
Benefit Concert for the Student Non-Violent Coordinating Committee
Newport Jazz Festival: Dave Brubeck Quartet/ Gerry Mulligan Big Band
Kool Jazz Festival: Friends of Alec Wilder

about_quotes.speaker: Tom Scott
about_quotes.evidence: So I go to the first rehearsal... there’s Mulligan and Brookmeyer and me... So here I am, this third guy... we go for a little while

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: The CTBB band – Clark Terry - Bob Brookmeyer band – started as a result of a mutual admiration society. about_quotes.speaker: David N. Baker
about_quotes.evidence: I was added to a trombone section that had Frank Rehak and Bobby Brookmeyer. ... I remember Bobby Brookmeyer and Frank Rehak came up to me and said, “Tell George these parts are almost impossible to play.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Kirchner
about_quotes.evidence: In 1989 and ’91 I spent two years studying with Bob Brookmeyer and Manny Albam in the BMI Jazz Composers’ Workshop. And they taught us a technique called “Pitch Modules”
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Kirchner interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 4, 2021

about_quotes.speaker: Manny Albam
about_quotes.evidence: And in the office was Billy Byers, Bob Brookmeyer, Al Cohn and myself... There was one Ray Charles thing which we all wrote under the name of some other arranger.
about_quotes.doc_title: Manny Albam interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Jerry Dodgion
about_quotes.evidence: we had one concert... Bob Brookmeyer sitting behind me... So we had everybody sit closer together... then the band started to sound better again
about_quotes.doc_title: Jerry Dodgion interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, March 9, 1996
